real_insight: The senior who has crossed the architecture-meme thicket evaluates each new proposal against actual constraints (read:write ratio, event volume, headcount) without bending the team's roadmap around the proposal. The diagnostic is whether the team's sprint plan moves with each new framework release.
notes: The engineering blog post vignettes (event-sourced CQRS on Kafka, FoundationDB) are recognisable as the kind of content that perturbs less-mature teams. The senior's response shape — read, map against actual constraints, two-line summary, return to PR queue — is operationally testable. The falsifiability clause is sharp: 'across six months, did the engineer adopt at least one new tool or pattern?' If no, what looks like nirveda is ossification. The counter-example carves out genuine adoption requirements (10x traffic, observability gap, auth deadline) — preventing the verse from becoming a default-no policy. Pass.
